question: What is the legal name of your company?
helperText: Provide the exact legal name as registered with the government authorities (e.g., Registrar of Companies).
guidanceTips: The company name should match the name on your GST registration, PAN card, and other official documents.
Avoid using abbreviations, acronyms, or informal names. Use the full legal entity name.
If your company has multiple locations, use the name of the headquarters or primary place of business.

question: What is your business registration number?
helperText: This is the unique identification number assigned to your business by the government. It is typically a combination of letters and numbers.
guidanceTips: The business registration number is typically a 10-15 digit alphanumeric code, such as a GST registration number or a PAN (Permanent Account Number).
Make sure to enter the exact number as it appears on your business registration documents, without any spaces or special characters.
If you are unsure of your business registration number, you can find it on official documents like your GST certificate, PAN card, or trade license.

question: What is your company's Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration number?
helperText: The GST number is a unique 15-digit identification number assigned to your business by the Indian government. It is mandatory for all businesses in India to have a valid GST registration.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for providing your GST number
guidanceTips: Ensure you enter the exact 15-digit GST number as it appears on your GST registration certificate.
The GST number follows a specific format: XX-AAAAA-NNNN-Z-Y-Z, where XX is the state code, AAAAA is the PAN-based business entity number, NNNN is a unique number, and Z and Y are check digits.
If you have recently registered for GST, make sure to use the new GST number and not the old VAT or service tax registration number.

question: What is your company's Permanent Account Number (PAN)?
helperText: The PAN is a unique 10-digit alphanumeric number issued by the Income Tax Department of India. It is mandatory for all businesses to have a valid PAN for tax and regulatory purposes.
guidanceTips: The PAN number should be entered exactly as it appears on your company's registration documents or tax filings.
Ensure that there are no typos or errors in the PAN number, as it is a critical identifier for your business.
If you do not have a PAN number yet, you will need to apply for one before proceeding with this insurance application.

question: What is the primary business activity or industry of your company?
helperText: Select the option that best describes your company's main business activity.
guidanceTips: Choose the industry that accounts for the majority of your company's revenue and operations.
If your business activities span multiple industries, select the one that is the primary focus.
For diversified companies, you may need to submit separate applications for each major business line.

question: What is the year your business was established?
helperText: Enter the 4-digit year your business was officially registered or started operations.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Year of Establishment
guidanceTips: The year of establishment should be the year your business was officially registered or started operations, not the year you started planning the business.
If you're not sure of the exact year, provide your best estimate based on official documents like your company registration certificate or first tax filings.
The year should be a 4-digit number between 1900 and the current year. Do not enter a range of years or leave out the century digit.

question: What is the total number of employees in your business?
placeholder: Enter the exact number of employees
helperText: Provide the current total number of employees in your business. This information is required to assess the risk profile for your trade credit insurance policy.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on Providing the Number of Employees
guidanceTips: Enter the exact number of full-time and part-time employees currently working in your business.
Include all employees, regardless of their role or department, when providing the total number.
Ensure the number you provide is accurate and up-to-date, as it will be used to determine the appropriate coverage and premium for your trade credit insurance policy.

question: What is your company's total annual revenue?
placeholder: Enter your annual revenue in INR
helperText: Please provide your company's total revenue for the last financial year. This information is required to assess your trade credit insurance needs.
guidanceTips: Enter the exact annual revenue figure, do not provide a range.
Ensure the revenue amount is for the most recent completed financial year.
If your company has multiple business verticals, provide the consolidated annual revenue across all operations.

question: What is the registered address of your business?
helperText: Provide the full address where your business operations are based, including the building name, street, locality, city, state, and PIN code.
guidanceTitle: Tips for Entering Your Business Address
guidanceTips: Make sure to include all relevant address details like building name, street, locality, city, state, and PIN code.
Ensure the address matches your company's registration documents like GST and PAN.
If your business has multiple locations, provide the address of your primary/registered office.

question: What is the city where your business is located?
helperText: Provide the exact city name where your business operations are based.
guidanceTips: Enter the full name of the city, not abbreviations or short forms.
Ensure the spelling is accurate as per official records.
If your business has multiple locations, provide the primary city of operation.
The city name should match your registered address for GST and PAN purposes.

question: In which state is your business registered?
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
helperText: Select the state where your business is registered.
guidanceTips: Ensure that the state selected matches the registered address of your business.
If your business has multiple locations, select the state where the primary/registered office is located.
The state selected should be consistent with the GST registration and other legal documents of your business.

question: What is the PIN code of your business location?
helperText: The 6-digit Postal Index Number (PIN) code is required to identify the location of your business. This is used for insurance coverage and claims processing.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering the PIN Code
guidanceTips: The PIN code is a 6-digit number assigned to a specific geographic area by the Indian postal department.
You can find your business PIN code by entering your address on the India Post website or by contacting your local post office.
Ensure you enter the correct 6-digit PIN code without any spaces or special characters.

question: Who is the primary contact person for this insurance policy?
placeholder: Enter full name of contact person
helperText: Provide the full name of the person who will be the primary point of contact for this insurance policy.
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the individual, including first, middle (if applicable), and last name.
Ensure the name matches the government-issued ID (such as PAN card or Aadhaar) of the contact person.
Avoid using abbreviations or nicknames in this field.

question: What is your official business email address?
helperText: Please provide the email address registered for your business. This will be used for all communication regarding your trade credit insurance policy.
guidanceTips: Use the official email address associated with your registered business, not a personal email.
Ensure the email address is spelled correctly and does not contain any typos.
If you have multiple business email addresses, provide the one you use for official correspondence.

question: What is your business's primary contact phone number?
placeholder: Enter your 10-digit mobile or landline number
helperText: Please provide a valid 10-digit phone number where you can be reached.
guidanceTips: Enter your primary business contact number, which can be a mobile or landline.
The number should be 10 digits long and start with 6, 7, 8, or 9.
Ensure the number provided is active and regularly monitored, as the insurance provider may need to contact you regarding your policy.

question: When would you like the trade credit insurance policy to start?
helperText: The policy period is typically 1 year. Please select a start date within the next 12 months.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for selecting the Policy Start Date
guidanceTips: The policy start date should be the date you want the coverage to begin.
It's best to choose a start date that aligns with your business operations and cash flow cycle.
Ensure the start date is not backdated, as insurers typically do not allow retroactive coverage.

question: When does the trade credit insurance policy need to end?
helperText: The policy period is typically 1 year. Enter the date when the current policy term should end.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering the Policy End Date
guidanceTips: The policy end date should be exactly 1 year after the policy start date.
Ensure the end date is within the allowed range of January 1, 2023 to December 31, 2024.
Double-check the date format is correct (DD/MM/YYYY).

question: What is the total value of your trade credit exposure that you would like to insure?
placeholder: Enter the total value in INR
helperText: This is the total value of your outstanding trade receivables that you would like to insure against non-payment by your customers.
guidanceTips: Consider your total annual sales and the average credit period extended to your customers.
Evaluate your top customers' credit risk and the potential impact of non-payment on your business.
Ensure the coverage amount is sufficient to protect your cash flow and working capital needs.
Keep in mind that the premium cost will be based on the total coverage amount selected.

question: Who is your current trade credit insurance provider?
placeholder: Enter the name of your current trade credit insurance provider
helperText: Provide the name of the insurance company that currently provides your trade credit insurance coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Current Insurance Provider
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the insurance company, not just an abbreviation or brand name.
If you do not currently have trade credit insurance, enter 'None' or 'Not Applicable'.
Ensure the information you provide is accurate, as it will be used to verify your existing coverage.

question: Please provide details of any previous insurance claims made by your business in the last 5 years.
helperText: Provide a brief summary of any insurance claims made, including the claim amount, date, and reason.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Previous Claims History
guidanceTips: Be as detailed as possible in describing the previous claims, including the policy type, claim amount, date of claim, and reason for the claim.
If your business has not made any previous insurance claims, please state 'No previous claims' in the field.
Providing accurate and complete information about your previous claims history is important for the insurer to assess the risk and provide an appropriate quote.

question: Please describe the risk management measures your business has in place to mitigate trade credit risks.
placeholder: Describe your risk management measures in detail
helperText: Provide information on credit checks, payment terms, debt collection processes, and any other measures you have implemented to manage trade credit risks.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on Effective Risk Management Measures
guidanceTips: Outline the specific processes and procedures your business has in place to assess the creditworthiness of your customers and manage outstanding receivables.
Explain how you conduct credit checks, set appropriate payment terms, and follow up on late payments to minimize the risk of bad debts.
Describe any insurance, factoring, or other financial instruments you use to transfer or mitigate trade credit risks.
